fork.commandNoMessages: Cannot fork: no messages in conversation
fork.commandNoAssistantUuid: Cannot fork: no assistant response with identifiers

redo.confirmTruncate: Redoing this response will discard all later messages in this chat. Continue?
redo.unavailableNoTurn: Cannot redo: matching user turn not found

messageActions.scrollToRecentUserAriaLabel: Scroll to most recent user message
messageActions.previousSameSpeakerAriaLabel: Jump to previous message from same speaker
messageActions.previousSameSpeakerUnavailable: No previous message from same speaker

inlineContext.selectTextFirst: Select text in a note first.
inlineContext.noActiveChatInput: Unable to find an active chat input.

composer.placeholder: How can I help you today?

errors.initAgentFailed: Failed to initialize agent service. Please try again.
errors.agentUnavailable: Agent service not available. Please reload the plugin.
errors.noTextSelected: No text selected in the active editor.
errors.templateVarsFailed: Failed to resolve template command variables.
errors.openMcpSettings: Open Pivi settings to manage MCP servers.

toolbar.externalAlreadyAdded: This folder is already added as an external context.

nav.scrollToRecentUser: Scroll to most recent user message
nav.previousSameSpeaker: Jump to previous message from same speaker

usage.unknownContextLength: Unable to determine the context length for the current model.

stream.escInterrupt: esc to interrupt · What should Pivi do instead?

image.unavailable: Image attachments are not available in the current runtime.

styleSettings.name: Style Settings
styleSettings.desc: Customize Pivi's appearance with the Style Settings plugin.
styleSettings.installationOpened: The Style Settings plugin page was opened. Install or enable it, then return to Integrations.

userName.name: What should Pivi call you?
userName.desc: Your name for personalized greetings (leave empty for generic greetings)

excludedTags.name: Excluded tags
excludedTags.desc: Notes with these tags will not auto-load as context (one per line, without #)

autoTitle.name: Auto-generate conversation titles
autoTitle.desc: Automatically generate conversation titles after the first user message is sent.

titleModel.name: Title generation model
titleModel.desc: Model used for auto-generating conversation titles.

autoCompact.name: Auto compact conversations
autoCompact.desc: Automatically summarize older session history after a response when context usage crosses the threshold.

threshold.name: Compact threshold
threshold.desc: Percentage of the current model context window that triggers automatic compaction.

keepRecent.name: Keep recent context
keepRecent.desc: Approximate tokens of recent session history to keep verbatim after compaction.

navMappings.name: Vim-style navigation mappings
navMappings.desc: One mapping per line. Format: "map <key> <action>" (actions: scrollUp, scrollDown, focusInput).

requireCommandOrControlEnterToSend.name: Require Command/Ctrl+Enter to send
requireCommandOrControlEnterToSend.desc: When enabled, Enter inserts a newline. Command+Enter sends on macOS; Ctrl+Enter sends on Windows and Linux.

noteToolbar.heading: Note Toolbar
noteToolbar.desc: Install or enable Note Toolbar when needed, then add Pivi's selection command to the toolbar shown for selected text.
noteToolbar.installed: Added Pivi to the selected-text toolbar.
noteToolbar.alreadyInstalled: Pivi is already in the selected-text toolbar.
noteToolbar.styleSettingsOpened: Pivi already uses the other style. Its Note Toolbar item settings were opened so you can update the label manually.
noteToolbar.pluginInstalledNeedsToolbar: Note Toolbar was installed. Choose a toolbar under Note Toolbar → Display locations → Selected text, then run setup again.
noteToolbar.needsToolbar: Choose a toolbar under Note Toolbar → Display locations → Selected text, then run setup again.
noteToolbar.installationOpened: The Note Toolbar plugin page was opened. Install or enable it, then run setup again.
noteToolbar.manualSetupOpened: Note Toolbar settings were opened. Add the Pivi command manually, or enable the official Obsidian CLI and retry.
noteToolbar.unsupportedVersion: Note Toolbar {version} does not support automatic setup. Update it and retry.
noteToolbar.invalidConfig: The selected-text toolbar configuration is invalid. Repair it in Note Toolbar settings and retry.
noteToolbar.verificationFailed: Note Toolbar reported success, but the Pivi command could not be verified.
noteToolbar.failed: Could not set up Note Toolbar: {message}

slashCommands.desc: Manage {workspaceName}-level custom commands stored in .pivi/commands/. Triggered by /name.

enableSpawn.name: Enable spawn_agent
enableSpawn.desc: Expose the spawn_agent tool so Pi can delegate focused subtasks to subagents.

allowBackground.name: Allow background subagents
allowBackground.desc: Allow spawn_agent calls with run_in_background=true. Completed results hydrate back into the subagent card.

maxConcurrent.name: Max simultaneous subagents
maxConcurrent.desc: Hard limit for concurrently running background subagents. Each spawn_agent call gets an isolated worker to avoid context cross-contamination.

tabBarPosition.name: Tab bar position
tabBarPosition.desc: Choose where to display tab badges and action buttons

enableAutoScroll.name: Auto-scroll during streaming
enableAutoScroll.desc: Automatically scroll to the bottom as Pi streams responses. Disable to stay at the top and read from the beginning.

deferMathRenderingDuringStreaming.name: Defer math rendering during streaming
deferMathRenderingDuringStreaming.desc: Show raw LaTeX while responses stream, then render math once when each text block completes.

chatViewPlacement.name: Open Pivi in
chatViewPlacement.desc: Choose where the chat panel opens when it is created

language.name: Language
language.desc: Change the display language of the plugin interface

deleteRemoved.name: Delete removed session files
deleteRemoved.desc: Permanently deletes only session files that were removed from history and are not archived or currently open.
deleteRemoved.failed: Failed to delete removed session files

sharedEnvironment.name: Environment variables
sharedEnvironment.desc: Environment variables for Pi runtime (KEY=VALUE format, one per line). Shell export prefix supported.

tools.heading: Tool toggles
tools.intro: Enable or disable {hostName} tools exposed to the agent. Changes apply to new turns after the agent prompt refreshes.
tools.unavailableExternalRead: Requires external read access and at least one allowed directory.

allowlist.name: Allowed bash commands
allowlist.desc: One command per line. Turn the bash tool on or off with the bash toggle under tool toggles. A bare executable such as git allows git subcommands; a line with spaces such as npm run build allows that exact command prefix. Basic lookup commands such as which, type, and pwd are always allowed. Shell control syntax is rejected.

externalRead.heading: External filesystem access

allow.name: Allow external file read/list
allow.desc: Allows Pivi to read and list files under the allowed external directories below, plus external context folders selected for the current chat session.

directories.name: Allowed external directories
directories.desc: One absolute directory per line. External read/list tools can only access paths inside these directories, plus external context folders selected for the current chat session.
directories.browseTooltip: Open a system folder picker and append the selected path
directories.alreadyAdded: This folder is already in the allowlist.

externalRead.needDirectory: Add at least one allowed external directory before external read tools become available.

webSearch.intro: Configure web tools. WebSearch can use Brave, Tavily, or Exa; WebFetch can use Tavily or Exa, with direct HTTP fallback.

preferredSearch.name: Preferred search provider
preferredSearch.desc: Used by WebSearch. Pivi tries this first, then falls back to other configured search providers and Exa public MCP.

preferredFetch.name: Preferred fetch provider
preferredFetch.desc: Used by WebFetch. Pivi tries this first, then falls back to other configured fetch-capable providers and direct HTTP.

webSearch.apiKeyDesc: Saved in {secureStorageName}. Used when provider is {provider} or auto.

modelsTab.intro: API keys and OAuth tokens are stored in {secureStorageName}. Disabled providers stay saved but are hidden from the model picker.
modelsTab.secureStorageRequired: Provider API keys require {hostName} {version} or newer with {secureStorageName}. Upgrade {hostName} to store credentials securely.
modelsTab.noModels: No models loaded yet. Fetch models from the endpoint, then enable them here.
modelsTab.displayNameDesc: Shown in the provider list and model picker grouping.
modelsTab.baseUrlDesc: API root including version path when required (for example /v1).
modelsTab.selectProvider: Please select a provider to add.
modelsTab.removeConfirmDescription: This removes the provider and its model selections. Its stored credential is kept by default.
modelsTab.removeCredential: Also delete this provider's credential from {secureStorageName}
modelsTab.readinessProviderUnavailable: Model readiness provider is not available.

statusDesc.missingCredential: Add an API key or supported OAuth credential to use this provider.
statusDesc.oauthExpired: An OAuth credential exists, but its expiry is in the past. Reconnect before using this provider.
statusDesc.disabled: Saved credentials are kept, but this provider is hidden from model selection.
statusDesc.unavailable: No local pi-ai model metadata is available for this provider yet.

codex.name: OpenAI Codex subscription
codex.desc: Sign in with your ChatGPT/Codex subscription. Credentials are stored in {secureStorageName}.
codex.notInitialized: Provider OAuth is not initialized. Reload the plugin.

modelsTab.apiKeyOptionalDesc: Optional. Leave empty for local servers that do not require auth. Saved in {secureStorageName} as {secretId} when set.

skills.intro: Agent skills live in .pivi/skills/ and are loaded on the next turn (skill tool + system prompt). Pivi installs or updates skills only after you choose an action below.

defaultBundle.name: Default {hostName} skills
defaultBundle.desc: Install the featured skills bundle for this {workspaceName}. This accesses GitHub/skills.sh, runs npx skills, and writes files under .pivi/skills/.
defaultBundle.label: Default bundle:
defaultBundle.installMore: Install more from skills.sh using owner/repo.

remote.name: Install from remote
remote.desc: Accepts owner/repo, GitHub URLs, git URLs, repo tree URLs, or local paths. First list remote skills, then choose which ones to install.
remote.heading: Remote skills

installed.heading: Installed skills
installed.empty: No skills in .pivi/skills/ yet. Install one below.

notices.noRemote: No remote skills found for this source.
notices.selectOne: Select at least one skill to install.

slashCommandsUi.heading: Custom commands
slashCommandsUi.empty: No custom commands yet. Add one to make it available from the / menu.
slashCommandsUi.catalogMissing: Slash command catalog is not initialized.

name.name: Command name
name.desc: The command slug used after / (e.g. explain, critique)

description.name: Description
description.desc: Description shown in the slash command autocomplete dropdown

argumentHint.name: Argument hint
argumentHint.desc: Hint text shown in the dropdown (e.g. code, text)

createCommand.needName: Please specify a valid command name.
createCommand.duplicate: A custom command named /{name} already exists.
createCommand.saveFailed: Failed to save the custom slash command.

mcp.heading: MCP servers
mcp.empty: No MCP servers configured. Click add to add one.
mcp.noValidConfig: No valid MCP configuration found in clipboard
mcp.saveReloadFailed: Setting saved but reload failed. Changes will apply on next session.
mcp.enterName: Enter a name for the server
mcp.contextSavingTitle: Slash badges: /{name} tokens highlight this server in the composer

modal.serverNameInvalid: Server name can only contain letters, numbers, dots, hyphens, and underscores

test.diskFull: Disk full. Free up space and try again.

commands.inlineEditUnavailableView: Inline edit unavailable: could not access the active Markdown view.

display.readDesc: Read note bodies by vault-relative path or wikilink-style file name.
display.editDesc: Replace exact text in existing notes. Preferred for partial edits.
display.writeDesc: Create notes, append/prepend content, or intentionally overwrite full notes.
display.searchDesc: Search note text, tags, or list markdown files in folders.
display.noteInfoDesc: Read metadata, tags, outgoing links, and frontmatter.
display.linksDesc: Read outgoing links or backlinks for a note.
display.propertiesDesc: List, read, set, or remove YAML frontmatter properties.
display.historyDesc: List, read, and restore Obsidian file history versions.
display.deleteDesc: Move vault files or folders to trash.
display.moveDesc: Rename or move vault files/folders and let Obsidian update links.
display.listDesc: List direct children of vault folders, including attachments.
display.readExternalDesc: Read files under allowed external directories by absolute path.
display.listExternalDesc: List direct children of allowed external directories by absolute path.
display.openDesc: Open a vault file in the Obsidian workspace.
display.attachmentDesc: Resolve attachment metadata/resource URLs or available attachment paths.
display.generateImageDesc: Generate images with Codex, save them as attachments, and optionally insert embeds into notes.
display.bashDesc: Run one-line shell commands when the Bash toggle is enabled and the command matches the allowlist configured above.
display.dailyDesc: Read, append, or prepend to the daily note, or resolve the daily note path. Requires daily-notes core plugin.
display.graphDesc: Analyze vault graph: orphans (no backlinks), deadends (no outgoing links), and unresolved (broken wikilinks).
display.tagsDesc: List all tags in the vault with counts, or get details for a single tag.
display.baseDesc: Query Obsidian Bases (built-in databases): list base files, inspect views, or query results. Only queries require the official Obsidian CLI.

inlineEdit.unavailableEditor: Inline edit unavailable: could not access the active editor. Try reopening the note.
inlineEdit.attachFailed: Failed to attach file: invalid path

host.failedSaveDeletedSessions: Failed to save deleted session list
